title: Motion In a Plane Test - 1 (Topic Test)

questions.questionText: A bird is flying in a direction 30° East of North with a speed of 5 kmh−1 and a cyclist is going on the road towards East at a speed of 10 kmh−1. The velocity of bird observed by cyclist is
options: 5√3 kmh−1 in a direction at 60° West of North
5√3 kmh−1 in a direction at 30° West of North
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two ships are 10 km apart on a line running from south to north. The one farther north is streaming towards west at 20 km hr−1. The other is streaming north at 20 km hr−1. The distance of closest approach is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A helicopter is flying south with a speed of 50 kmh−1. A train is moving with the same speed towards east. The relative velocity of the helicopter as seen by the passengers in the train will be towards :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A ship is travelling due east at 10 km/h. A ship 30° east of north is always due north from the first ship. The speed of the second ship in km/h is -
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: For four particles A, B, C & D the velocities of one with respect to other are given as $\vec{v}_{DC}$ is 20 m/s towards north, $\vec{v}_{BC}$ is 20 m/s towards east and $\vec{v}_{BA}$ is 20 m/s towards south. Then $\vec{v}_{DA}$ is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two persons P and Q start from points A and B respectively as shown in figure. P and Q have speed $v = 12\,\text{m/s}$ in shown directions towards point O. When the distance between P and Q is $120\,\text{m}$, then Q increases its speed to $15\,\text{m/s}$. Then find out who will reach the point O first.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Two aeroplanes fly from their respective positions 'A' and 'B' starting at the same time and reach the point 'C' simultaneously when wind was not blowing. On a windy day they head towards 'C' but both reach the point 'D' simultaneously in the same time which they took to reach 'C'. Then the wind is blowing in
options: Direction making an angle $0 < \theta < 90$ with North towards East.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A man walking eastward at $5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ observes that wind is blowing from the north. On doubling his speed eastward, he observes that wind is blowing from north-east. Find the velocity of the wind.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The relative velocity of a car 'A' with respect to car B is $30\sqrt{2}$ m/s due North-East. The velocity of car 'B' is 20 m/s due south. The relative velocity of car 'C' with respect to car 'A' is $10\sqrt{2}$ m/s due North-West. The speed of car C and the direction (in terms of angle it makes with the east) are
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A bird is moving northward with speed 40 km h–1. A train is moving eastward with speed 40 km h–1. Find the velocity of the bird as observed by a person sitting in the train.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Airplanes $A$ and $B$ are flying with constant velocity in the same vertical plane respectively at angle $30^{\circ}$ and $60^{\circ}$ with respect to the horizontal as shown in the figure. The speed of $A$ is $100 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$. At time $t=$ 0 s , an observer in $A$ finds $B$ at a distance of 500 m . This observer sees $B$ moving with a constant velocity perpendicular to the line of motion of $A$. If at $t=t_0$, $A$ just escapes being hit by $B$, then $t_0$ in seconds is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is projected at angle $60^{\circ}$ with speed $10 \sqrt{3}$, from the point ' $A$ ' as shown in the fig. At the same time the wedge is made to move with speed $10 \sqrt{3}$ towards right as shown in the figure. If the time after which particle strikes with wedge is $\frac{\mathrm{N}}{13}$ seconds, then find out the value of $\mathrm{N} .\left(\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}^2\right)$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: CE and DF are two walls of equal height ( 20 meter) from which two particles $A$ and $B$ of same mass are projected as shown in the figure. A is projected horizontally towards left while B is projected at an angle $37^{\circ}$ (with horizontal towards left) with velocity $15 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{sec}$. If $A$ always sees $B$ to be moving perpendicular to $E F$, then find out the range of $A$ on ground in meter.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Ship $A$ moves with a speed of $5 \mathrm{~km} \mathrm{~h}^{-1}$ towards the east. Another ship is moving along a direction $30^{\circ}$ east of north. What must be the speed of the second ship so that, relative to ship $A$, it always stays exactly due north?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Consider two particles, denoted as 1 and 2. Their initial position vectors at time $t=0$ are given as $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}_1=5 \hat{\mathrm{i}}+2 \hat{\mathrm{j}} \mathrm{m}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}_2=-4 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-4 \hat{\mathrm{j}} \mathrm{m}$, while they move with constant velocities $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}}_1=4 \hat{\mathrm{i}}-3 \hat{\mathrm{j}} \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}}_2=b \hat{\mathrm{i}}-\hat{\mathrm{j}} \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$. If both particles undergo a collision at $t=3 \mathrm{~s}$, then the numerical value of constant $b$ is:
questions.subject: Physics
